'''Iain Thornley''' (born 11 September 1991) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} and {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales Scorpions]] in [[2013 Championship 1|Championship 1]], [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] and [[Workington Town]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League. Thornley has also played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League|top flight]].

Between 2009 and 2012 Thornley played [[rugby union]] with the [[Sale Sharks]] and [[Leeds Carnegie]].

==Background==
Thornley was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

==Career==
Signed to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] as a teenager he was selected for the England Academy side but at age 18, he switched codes to [[Rugby Union]] to join [[Sale Sharks]].<ref>{{cite news|url= https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_union/my_club/sale/8842387.stm|title= Sale Sharks sign Iain Thornley from Wigan Warriors|work=BBC Sport|date= 21 July 2010|access-date= 7 April 2013 }}</ref>  After two seasons Thornley rejoined Wigan on a three-year contract.<ref>{{cite news|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18004347|title=Iain Thornley re-joins Wigan Warriors from Sale Sharks|work=BBC Sport|date= 9 May 2012|access-date = 7 April 2013 }}</ref>
He played in the [[2013 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23794947|title= Hull FC 0-16 Wigan Warriors|date=24 August 2013|work= BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/aug/24/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final|title= Wigan Warriors overcome Hull FC and elements to win Challenge Cup|date=24 August 2013|work= Guardian|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3660839,00.html|title= Wigan Warriors grind out victory over Hull|date=24 August 2013|work= Sky Sports|access-date=13 November 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140203195127/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3660839,00.html|archive-date=3 February 2014}}</ref>
He played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

===Wigan (re-join)===
On 17 November 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Nov 2021|accessdate=24 Dec 2021|title=Wigan confirm two new signings|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-confirm-two-new-signings-iain-thornley-ramon-silva/}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, he played for Wigan in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }}</ref>
In round 11 of the 2023 Super League season, Thornley made his first appearance for Wigan in eleven months and scored two tries for the club in their 14-10 loss against [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-nail-rare-traits-8414168/|title=Hull FC nail rare traits in victory as Jake Trueman shows dark arts on debut|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=5 May 2023|archive-date=16 May 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230516185110/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-nail-rare-traits-8414168|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 25 October 2023, it was confirmed that he would join [[Wakefield Trinity]] for the 2024 season on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Live|date=25 Oct 2023|title=Wakefield Trinity secure capture of Wigan Warriors centre as squad rebuild starts|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-secure-capture-wigan-27983814.amp}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 12 November 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Nov 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Championship new boys sign former Super League and Challenge Cup winner|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/championship-oldham-sign-ex-wigan-warriors-wakefield-trinity-iain-thornley}}</ref>
